mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 10 percent signals severe labor market distress and collapsing consumer demand, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 10 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that drives corporate earnings upward, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative or zero average 60-day forward return, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month rebounds in industrial production following a severe contraction indicate supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds fail to generate positive 20-day forward returns during documented recovery phases, or the signal performs worse than a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, monetary policy reaches the effective lower bound, forcing markets to price in aggressive fiscal stimulus and liquidity injections that boost equities.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain flat or negative during periods where FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25 percent, contradicting the liquidity premium hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates signals labor market tightening and wage inflation pressures that compress corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 60-day forward returns in over 60 percent of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts during late-cycle expansions reflect peak consumer confidence and credit availability, which precedes a demand cliff and generates negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts acceleration correlates with positive equity returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal shows no predictive power outside early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 5 percent indicates a sudden supply shock that disrupts manufacturing output and triggers immediate negative market repricing.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines more than 5 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances following a 5 percent monthly drop in industrial production.
